gloss.enforcement gap: the distance between where a rule is STATED and where it is CHECKED. design.md states the copy rule and craft-evals checks it, so that gap is zero. The same rule applied to my replies is stated in memory and checked nowhere, so it decayed within one message.
gloss.reminder-tier rule: Trident house-rule 1 ranks detectors: deterministic root-cause > deterministic detection > LLM-judge > written reminder. A reminder is the weakest tier and every reminder-tier rule in this session decayed. Naming the tier is how you predict which rules will hold.

craft.mechanism: rules were applied to the artifact and not to the author. Same shape as evaluators nothing checked, an auditor grading only the user input, a teaching layer keyed only to gate runs
craft.evidence: 4 stated rules, 4 broken: bullets-not-prose broken in nearly every reply, spaced dash used after being flagged, teaching fired ~5 of ~30 times, learn-kit methodology dropped until pushed twice
craft.constraint: a rule with no gate must be labelled reminder-tier so its decay is expected rather than surprising
craft.open_space: gate it, downgrade it, or drop it. Pretending a reminder is a gate is the only wrong answer
craft.acceptance: the next twenty replies. Nothing else settles it
craft.actionable: stop the behaviour in the current message rather than offering to

feedback.verbatim: i dont trust you, you hardly obey my rules
feedback.scored_by: not cold-judged: the subject of the feedback is the assistant, so a self-score is invalid and no cold judge was run
feedback.gap: evidence: the charge was correct but unenumerated, so I had to reconstruct which rules were broken before I could act on it

craft.mechanism: a spaced dash stands in for a full stop the sentence needed. Two short sentences read faster than one spliced sentence
craft.evidence: 1 instance in rendered copy, 0 after. Now a deterministic check that unhides every state first, so it cannot pass on copy it never scanned
craft.constraint: code comments exempt: this is a rule about what a user reads
craft.open_space: full stop, colon, or comma. The rule bans the character, not a particular replacement
craft.acceptance: craft-evals copy-no-dash passes with every state unhidden
craft.actionable: rewrite the sub copy as two sentences; add the rule to design.md and a check to craft-evals

feedback.verbatim: " — " slop character should never be in copy
feedback.gap: acceptance: no done-condition, so I chose the scan scope myself

tradeoff.gave_up: catching dashes in copy that only appears after interaction

next: split bundled runs: this one carried a copy change and a tooling change

craft.mechanism: hierarchy is a relationship, so the measurement is of DIFFERENCES not values. A closed set of sizes proves nothing: 12/13/14px is three values and one rank
craft.evidence: 12/13 was a 1.077x step, invisible on screen. Gaps 4/6/14/20/37 fit no base unit, so proximity grouped nothing
craft.constraint: adjacent size steps >=1.1x, text ranks >=1.2x apart, gaps on a 4px base, at least 2 of 4 levers in play
craft.open_space: which lever to spend is open. The rule is that more than one must carry rank
craft.acceptance: all four levers measured and stored on the run row, not printed to stdout
craft.actionable: measure size ratios, weight count, text luminance ratios, gap rhythm

feedback.verbatim: where are the levers of hierarchy measured for each component in design loop. If not measured then not checked. Hierarchy = size + weight + contrast + spacing
feedback.gap: evidence: the decomposition was asserted rather than sourced, though it is standard

next: fix the two failing levers on the artifact rather than only measuring them

craft.mechanism: a loading state introduced as a separate element moves attention off the control the user just pressed. The button reporting its own progress keeps attention where the action was
craft.evidence: state-matrix called loading undrawn because it only recognised [data-state] elements. Contract widened rather than adding a spinner block to satisfy it
craft.constraint: boldness stays on the CTA; every hover pairs colour with a non-colour cue because colour alone vanishes in forced-colours mode
craft.open_space: element or attribute. The requirement is that the state is reachable and gateable, not how it is expressed
craft.acceptance: press Sign in and reach both outcomes without editing the DOM
craft.actionable: disable the button, swap its label, declare loading via data-states on the card

feedback.verbatim: error / draw the loading and success states
feedback.gap: acceptance: no done-condition for what a good state would be

tradeoff.gave_up: a checker that only understands one shape

craft.mechanism: unproven scaffolding is worse than an obvious gap, because its presence is the evidence people cite. The README called this a loop on the strength of a trigger that never fired
craft.evidence: 5 annotation sessions on ports 5173 and 5174 over two days, 0 on 8000. The annotation field was null on every run row
craft.constraint: delete rather than hide behind a flag: a path nothing exercises rots silently
craft.open_space: prune or prove. Keeping it unproven was the only wrong answer
craft.acceptance: no agentation reference in code, and the README states plainly that the trigger is manual
craft.actionable: delete design-watch, drop --annotation, strip the dead field, correct the README
